summary: Add web-to-desktop authentication handoff so rust-diffforge opens the hosted web login, receives a deep-link callback, and resumes with a revocable desktop session.

review_items.summary: Next.js adds a /desktop/login handoff page plus desktop auth APIs that create one-time auth codes from verified Appwrite web sessions.
Tauri validates saved sessions on app startup, opens the hosted web login only after the user presses the sign-in button, handles diffforge://auth/callback deep links, and exchanges one-time codes.
Add short-lived one-time auth codes for browser-to-desktop handoff. Codes are stored only as hashes, expire quickly, and can be consumed once.
Add revocable desktop session records linked to users. Session bearer tokens are stored only as hashes and expire after a bounded window.
Create desktop_auth_codes and desktop_sessions tables with uniqueness, ownership, expiry, and lookup indexes.
Deep links carry only one-time codes, not long-lived session tokens. Desktop must match the returned state before exchanging a code.
Add /api/desktop/auth-codes, /api/desktop/sessions/exchange, and /api/desktop/session contracts for create, exchange, validate, and revoke behavior.
Desktop session exchange and validation responses include user.planStatus derived from server billing state so rust-diffforge can gate dashboard access.

security_data_rules: Desktop auth code creation requires a verified Appwrite JWT from an existing web session.
Desktop auth codes and desktop session tokens are never stored raw in SQL; only SHA-256 hashes are persisted.
The deep-link callback must include a state value generated by the desktop app and must match the locally pending state before exchange.
One-time auth codes expire after five minutes and cannot be exchanged after consumed_at is set.
Desktop sessions expire after thirty days, are revocable, and must be validated by the server before the desktop app treats a stored token as authenticated.
The desktop app should clear local session state when validation fails or logout succeeds.
The desktop app should not initiate a new browser login on startup; only an explicit user action may create a pending auth state and open the hosted web login.
Desktop startup session validation should not block the user from pressing the web sign-in button.
Desktop session responses must derive user.planStatus from server-owned billing state and treat only active or trialing Stripe subscriptions as paid.
The web sign-in returnTo parameter must be same-origin path-only to avoid open redirects.

cross_project_impact.contract: Web login at https://diffforge.ai/desktop/login plus diffforge://auth/callback deep link and /api/desktop/* session APIs.
cross_project_impact.compatibility_notes: Existing browser sign-in, dashboard, billing, and Stripe webhook behavior remains compatible. The new desktop APIs add new consumers only.

safe_deploy_order: Apply SQL migration for desktop_auth_codes and desktop_sessions.
Deploy next-diffforge desktop auth APIs and /desktop/login page.
Ship rust-diffforge app with deep-link handler and session exchange support.
compatibility.notes: The desktop app cannot complete web login until both the SQL migration and Next.js auth endpoints are deployed.

tests.summary: Validate policy JSON and SQL graph references.
Validate Next.js auth route and page code.
Validate Tauri host, deep-link, and session API commands.
